Verbatim_Snippet: The purpose of this Act is to govern the collection, use and disclosure of personal data by organisations in a manner that recognises both the right of individuals to protect their personal data and the need of organisations to collect, use or disclose personal data for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate in the circumstances.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision sets out the fundamental purpose of the Act, establishing a comprehensive data protection framework that governs the collection, use, and disclosure of personal data across the private sector. It balances individual privacy rights with organisational needs, serving as the statutory foundation for the Act's extensive regime covering consent, data subject rights, data breach notification, and enforcement mechanisms.

Verbatim_Snippet: An Act to amend the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (Act 26 of 2012) and to make consequential and related amendments to certain other Acts.
Mapping_Rationale: This Act amends the Personal Data Protection Act 2012, which serves as Singapore's comprehensive data protection framework. The amendments significantly expand and strengthen the existing framework by introducing mandatory data breach notification, data portability rights, and enhanced enforcement and financial penalty mechanisms.
Impact_or_comments:
Long Title: An Act to amend the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (Act 26 of 2012) and to make consequential and related amendments to certain other Acts.
Interpretation: This Act amends the Personal Data Protection Act 2012, which serves as Singapore's comprehensive data protection framework. The amendments significantly expand and strengthen the existing framework by introducing mandatory data breach notification, data portability rights, and enhanced enforcement and financial penalty mechanisms.

Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a dedicated, cross-cutting cybersecurity framework by empowering the Commissioner of Cybersecurity to oversee national cybersecurity, designate and regulate Critical Information Infrastructure (CII), and mandate codes of practice and performance standards. It forms the statutory basis for security measures, incident response, and CII protection across all essential sectors in Singapore.

Verbatim_Snippet: Subject to subsection (2), any person who knowingly causes a computer to perform any function for the purpose of securing access without authority to any program or data held in any computer shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction — (a) to a fine not exceeding $5,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years or to both; and (b) in the case of a second or subsequent conviction, to a fine not exceeding $10,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years or to both.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes the foundational computer misuse offence by criminalising unauthorised access to computer material, which forms a core component of a dedicated cybersecurity framework. By penalising unauthorised access, the law provides a baseline legal mechanism to protect computer systems and data from cyber threats and intrusions.
Impact_or_comments:
Section 3(1): Subject to subsection (2), any person who knowingly causes a computer to perform any function for the purpose of securing access without authority to any program or data held in any computer shall be guilty of an offence and shall be liable on conviction — (a) to a fine not exceeding $5,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years or to both; and (b) in the case of a second or subsequent conviction, to a fine not exceeding $10,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years or to both.
Interpretation: This provision establishes the foundational computer misuse offence by criminalising unauthorised access to computer material, which forms a core component of a dedicated cybersecurity framework. By penalising unauthorised access, the law provides a baseline legal mechanism to protect computer systems and data from cyber threats and intrusions.

Verbatim_Snippet: 1. The following cybersecurity services are licensable cybersecurity services for the purposes of this Act: (a) managed security operations centre (SOC) monitoring service; (b) penetration testing service.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision forms part of the dedicated national cybersecurity framework by establishing a licensing regime for critical cybersecurity services, specifically managed security operations centre monitoring and penetration testing. It ensures that entities providing these specialized security services are regulated to maintain national cybersecurity standards.
Impact_or_comments:

Verbatim_Snippet: An organisation must retain records relating to an investigation under this section for one year after the conclusion of the investigation or any longer period specified in writing by the Commission.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a minimum data retention period of one year for records relating to an investigation conducted under the Act, or longer if specified by the Commission. This ensures that organizations preserve investigation-related records for regulatory oversight and potential enforcement actions, creating a mandatory retention floor that operates alongside the general data minimization principles.
Impact_or_comments:

Verbatim_Snippet:
22A. —(1) Where —
(a) an individual, on or after the date of commencement of section 11 of the Personal Data Protection (Amendment) Act 2020, makes a request under section 21(1)(a) to an organisation to provide personal data about the individual that is in the possession or under the control of the organisation; and
(b) the organisation refuses to provide that personal data,
the organisation must preserve, for not less than the prescribed period, a copy of the personal data concerned.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision requires organisations to preserve a copy of personal data for not less than the prescribed period when an individual's access request is refused. It establishes a conditional minimum data retention period to ensure the data is available for potential review or dispute resolution regarding the refusal.

Act_and_or_practice: Licence to Provide Facilities-Based Operations Granted by the Info-communications Media Development Authority to Singapore Telecommunications Limited under Section 5 of the Telecommunications Act (Chapter 323)
Verbatim_Snippet: All data records shall be kept by the Licensee for a period of not less than twelve (12) calendar months.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision mandates a minimum data retention period of twelve calendar months for all data records associated with International Simple Resale services. It establishes a sector-specific record-keeping obligation for telecommunications licensees to ensure data availability for regulatory and law enforcement inspection.
Impact_or_comments:
Schedule C, Condition 5.3: All data records shall be kept by the Licensee for a period of not less than twelve (12) calendar months.
Interpretation: This provision mandates a minimum data retention period of twelve calendar months for all data records associated with International Simple Resale services. It establishes a sector-specific record-keeping obligation for telecommunications licensees to ensure data availability for regulatory and law enforcement inspection.

Verbatim_Snippet: An organisation must designate one or more individuals to be responsible for ensuring that the organisation complies with this Act.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision mandates the appointment of a Data Protection Officer or designated individual responsible for ensuring organisational compliance with the Act. Additionally, the law requires data protection impact assessments for specific processing activities, such as deemed consent by notification and legitimate interests, to identify and mitigate adverse effects on individuals.
Impact_or_comments:
Section 11(3): An organisation must designate one or more individuals to be responsible for ensuring that the organisation complies with this Act.
Interpretation: This provision mandates the appointment of a Data Protection Officer or designated individual responsible for ensuring organisational compliance with the Act. Additionally, the law requires data protection impact assessments for specific processing activities, such as deemed consent by notification and legitimate interests, to identify and mitigate adverse effects on individuals.

Verbatim_Snippet: An organisation shall designate one or more individuals to be responsible for ensuring that the organisation complies with this Act.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a mandatory Data Protection Officer (DPO) requirement by obliging organisations to designate one or more individuals responsible for ensuring compliance with the Act. The law does not include any provisions requiring the conduct of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs).
Impact_or_comments:

The law does not include any provisions requiring the conduct of Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs).

Act_and_or_practice: Guide to Data Protection Impact Assessments
Verbatim_Snippet: In deciding on the policies and practices to be implemented in compliance with the PDPA, organisations are encouraged to conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment ("DPIA")
Mapping_Rationale: The provided text is a regulatory guide rather than primary legislation, stating that organisations are 'encouraged' to conduct a DPIA to meet their obligations under the PDPA, rather than imposing a strict statutory mandate. It also references the Data Protection Officer (DPO) as the person responsible for enforcing data protection policies and leading the DPIA, reflecting the broader accountability framework of the PDPA.
Impact_or_comments:
INTRODUCTION: In deciding on the policies and practices to be implemented in compliance with the PDPA, organisations are encouraged to conduct a Data Protection Impact Assessment ("DPIA")
Interpretation: The provided text is a regulatory guide rather than primary legislation, stating that organisations are 'encouraged' to conduct a DPIA to meet their obligations under the PDPA, rather than imposing a strict statutory mandate. It also references the Data Protection Officer (DPO) as the person responsible for enforcing data protection policies and leading the DPIA, reflecting the broader accountability framework of the PDPA.

Mapping_Rationale: This provision establishes a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) requirement, mandating organisations to conduct an assessment to identify and mitigate adverse effects on individuals before relying on deemed consent by notification for data collection, use, or disclosure. It ensures that potential harms from processing activities are evaluated and addressed proactively.

Verbatim_Snippet:
(1A) Without limiting subsection (1), where a police officer of or above the rank of sergeant, or an authorised person, considers that any data (other than data in the custody of the Postal Authority, a public postal licensee or the public parcel locker network operator) is necessary or desirable for any investigation, inquiry, trial or other proceeding under this Code, the police officer or authorised person may — (a) issue a written order to require a person in whose possession or power the data is believed to be — (i) to authenticate the data; and (ii) to produce the data, at the time and place stated in the order; or (b) in the case of any data that is contained in or available to a computer — issue a written order to require a person who is believed to have power to access the data from that computer — (i) to authenticate a copy of the data; and (ii) to produce a copy of the data, at the time and place stated in the order.

(5) A police officer, or an authorised person, may exercise the powers conferred under this section despite any provision in any other law relating to the production of, or the giving of any access to, any document or thing, or data.
Mapping_Rationale: This provision grants law enforcement authorities the power to issue written orders compelling individuals or organizations to produce, authenticate, or provide access to data for criminal investigations. Subsection (5) explicitly allows these powers to be exercised notwithstanding any provisions in other laws (such as data protection legislation) regarding the production of or access to data, establishing a clear legal exception for government access in the context of criminal proceedings.
Impact_or_comments:

Verbatim_Snippet: (n) the personal data is disclosed to any officer of a prescribed law enforcement agency, upon production of written authorisation signed by the head or director of that law enforcement agency or a person of a similar rank, certifying that the personal data is necessary for the purposes of the functions or duties of the officer;
Mapping_Rationale: This provision allows organisations to disclose personal data to prescribed law enforcement officers without the individual's consent, provided the agency head issues written authorisation certifying its necessity for their duties. It creates a conditional exception to data protection rules, facilitating government access for law enforcement while imposing a formal internal authorization requirement.
Impact_or_comments:
